lines.gurmukhi: ਜਨੁ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਤਿਨ ਕਉ ਵਾਰਿਆ ਸਦਾ ਸਦਾ ਕੁਰਬਾਣੀ ॥੧੦॥
lines.translit: jan naanak tin kau vaariaa sadaa sadaa kurabaanee |10|
lines.english: Servant Nanak is a sacrifice to them; He is forever and ever a sacrifice. ||10||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਗੁਰੁ ਸੇਵਦੇ ਸੇ ਪੁੰਨ ਪਰਾਣੀ ॥
lines.translit: jo gurasikh gur sevade se pun paraanee |
lines.english: Those Sikhs of the Guru, who serve the Guru, are the most blessed beings.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਖੀ ਸਹੇਲੀਆ ਸੇ ਆਪਿ ਹਰਿ ਭਾਈਆ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh sakhee saheleea se aap har bhaaeea |
lines.english: The Lord Himself is pleased with the Gurmukhs, the fellowship of the companions.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਦਰਗਹ ਪੈਨਾਈਆ ਹਰਿ ਆਪਿ ਗਲਿ ਲਾਈਆ ॥੧੧॥
lines.translit: har daragah painaaeea har aap gal laaeea |11|
lines.english: In the Lord's Court, they are given robes of honor, and the Lord Himself hugs them close in His embrace. ||11||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਧਿਆਇਦੇ ਤਿਨ ਦਰਸਨੁ ਦੀਜੈ ॥
lines.translit: jo guramukh naam dhiaaeide tin darasan deejai |
lines.english: Please bless me with the Blessed Vision of the Darshan of those Gurmukhs, who meditate on the Naam, the Name of the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਮ ਤਿਨ ਕੇ ਚਰਣ ਪਖਾਲਦੇ ਧੂੜਿ ਘੋਲਿ ਘੋਲਿ ਪੀਜੈ ॥੧੨॥
lines.translit: ham tin ke charan pakhaalade dhoorr ghol ghol peejai |12|
lines.english: I wash their feet, and drink in the dust of their feet, dissolved in the wash water. ||12||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਕਦੇ ਨ ਚੇਤਿਓ ਜਮਿ ਪਕੜਿ ਚਲਾਈਆ ॥੧੩॥
lines.translit: har har kade na chetio jam pakarr chalaaeea |13|
lines.english: but do not contemplate the Lord, Har, Har - the Messenger of Death will seize them and take them away. ||13||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਨ ਸੁਪਾਰੀ ਖਾਤੀਆ ਮੁਖਿ ਬੀੜੀਆ ਲਾਈਆ ॥
lines.translit: paan supaaree khaateea mukh beerreea laaeea |
lines.english: Those who eat betel nuts and betel leaf and smoke intoxicants,

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮਾ ਹਰਿ ਚੇਤਿਆ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਉਰਿ ਧਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: jin har naamaa har chetiaa hiradai ur dhaare |
lines.english: The Messenger of Death does not even approach those who contemplate the Name of the Lord, Har, Har,

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨ ਜਮੁ ਨੇੜਿ ਨ ਆਵਈ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਗੁਰ ਪਿਆਰੇ ॥੧੪॥
lines.translit: tin jam nerr na aavee gurasikh gur piaare |14|
lines.english: And keep Him enshrined in their hearts. The Guru's Sikhs are the Guru's Beloveds. ||14||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕਾ ਨਾਮੁ ਨਿਧਾਨੁ ਹੈ ਕੋਈ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਜਾਣੈ ॥
lines.translit: har kaa naam nidhaan hai koee guramukh jaanai |
lines.english: The Name of the Lord is a treasure, known only to the few Gurmukhs.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਜਿਨ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਭੇਟਿਆ ਰੰਗਿ ਰਲੀਆ ਮਾਣੈ ॥੧੫॥
lines.translit: naanak jin satigur bhettiaa rang raleea maanai |15|
lines.english: O Nanak, those who meet with the True Guru, enjoy peace and pleasure. ||15||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਗੁਰ ਵਿਟਹੁ ਸਦ ਵਾਰਿਆ ਜਿਨਿ ਦਿਤੜਾ ਨਾਓ ॥੧੬॥
lines.translit: hau gur vittahu sad vaariaa jin ditarraa naao |16|
lines.english: I am forever a sacrifice to the Guru, who has blessed me with the Lord's Name. ||16||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਦਾਤਾ ਆਖੀਐ ਤੁਸਿ ਕਰੇ ਪਸਾਓ ॥
lines.translit: satigur daataa aakheeai tus kare pasaao |
lines.english: The True Guru is called the Giver; in His Mercy, He grants His Grace.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਵੇਖਿ ਵੇਖਿ ਗੁਰੂ ਵਿਗਸਿਆ ਗੁਰ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਦੇਹਾ ॥੧੭॥
lines.translit: hau vekh vekh guroo vigasiaa gur satigur dehaa |17|
lines.english: I gaze upon the Guru, the Guru, the True Guru embodied, and I blossom forth in bliss. ||17||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਧੰਨੁ ਗੁਰੂ ਸਾਬਾਸਿ ਹੈ ਹਰਿ ਦੇਇ ਸਨੇਹਾ ॥
lines.translit: so dhan guroo saabaas hai har dee sanehaa |
lines.english: Blessed, very blessed is the Guru, who brings the Lord's message.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰ ਰਸਨਾ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਬੋਲਦੀ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮਿ ਸੁਹਾਵੀ ॥
lines.translit: gur rasanaa amrit boladee har naam suhaavee |
lines.english: The Guru's tongue recites Words of Ambrosial Nectar; He is adorned with the Lord's Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਸੁਣਿ ਸਿਖਾ ਗੁਰੁ ਮੰਨਿਆ ਤਿਨਾ ਭੁਖ ਸਭ ਜਾਵੀ ॥੧੮॥
lines.translit: jin sun sikhaa gur maniaa tinaa bhukh sabh jaavee |18|
lines.english: Those Sikhs who hear and obey the Guru - all their desires depart. ||18||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕਾ ਮਾਰਗੁ ਆਖੀਐ ਕਹੁ ਕਿਤੁ ਬਿਧਿ ਜਾਈਐ ॥
lines.translit: har kaa maarag aakheeai kahu kit bidh jaaeeai |
lines.english: Some speak of the Lord's Path; tell me, how can I walk on it?

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਤੇਰਾ ਨਾਮੁ ਹੈ ਹਰਿ ਖਰਚੁ ਲੈ ਜਾਈਐ ॥੧੯॥
lines.translit: har har teraa naam hai har kharach lai jaaeeai |19|
lines.english: O Lord, Har, Har, Your Name is my supplies; I will take it with me and set out. ||19||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹਰਿ ਆਰਾਧਿਆ ਸੇ ਸਾਹ ਵਡ ਦਾਣੇ ॥
lines.translit: jin guramukh har aaraadhiaa se saah vadd daane |
lines.english: Those Gurmukhs who worship and adore the Lord, are wealthy and very wise.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕਉ ਸਦ ਵਾਰਿਆ ਗੁਰ ਬਚਨਿ ਸਮਾਣੇ ॥੨੦॥
lines.translit: hau satigur kau sad vaariaa gur bachan samaane |20|
lines.english: I am forever a sacrifice to the True Guru; I am absorbed in the Words of the Guru's Teachings. ||20||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂ ਠਾਕੁਰੁ ਤੂ ਸਾਹਿਬੋ ਤੂਹੈ ਮੇਰਾ ਮੀਰਾ ॥
lines.translit: too tthaakur too saahibo toohai meraa meeraa |
lines.english: You are the Master, my Lord and Master; You are my Ruler and King.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਧੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਤੇਰੀ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਤੂ ਗੁਣੀ ਗਹੀਰਾ ॥੨੧॥
lines.translit: tudh bhaavai teree bandagee too gunee gaheeraa |21|
lines.english: If it is pleasing to Your Will, then I worship and serve You; You are the treasure of virtue. ||21||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਹਰਿ ਇਕ ਰੰਗੁ ਹੈ ਆਪੇ ਬਹੁ ਰੰਗੀ ॥
lines.translit: aape har ik rang hai aape bahu rangee |
lines.english: The Lord Himself is absolute; He is The One and Only; but He Himself is also manifested in many forms.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਤਿਸੁ ਭਾਵੈ ਨਾਨਕਾ ਸਾਈ ਗਲ ਚੰਗੀ ॥੨੨॥੨॥
lines.translit: jo tis bhaavai naanakaa saaee gal changee |22|2|
lines.english: Whatever pleases Him, O Nanak, that alone is good. ||22||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਲੰਗ ਮਹਲਾ ੯ ਕਾਫੀ ॥
lines.translit: tilang mahalaa 9 kaafee |
lines.english: Tilang, Ninth Mehl, Kaafee:

lines.gurmukhi: ਛਿਨੁ ਛਿਨੁ ਅਉਧ ਬਿਹਾਤੁ ਹੈ ਫੂਟੈ ਘਟ ਜਿਉ ਪਾਨੀ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: chhin chhin aaudh bihaat hai foottai ghatt jiau paanee |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Each and every moment, your life is passing away, like water from a cracked pitcher. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਚੇਤਨਾ ਹੈ ਤਉ ਚੇਤ ਲੈ ਨਿਸਿ ਦਿਨਿ ਮੈ ਪ੍ਰਾਨੀ ॥
lines.translit: chetanaa hai tau chet lai nis din mai praanee |
lines.english: If you are conscious, then be conscious of Him night and day, O mortal.

lines.gurmukhi: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ॥
lines.translit: ik oankaar satigur prasaad |
lines.english: One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਗੁਨ ਕਾਹਿ ਨ ਗਾਵਹੀ ਮੂਰਖ ਅਗਿਆਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: har gun kaeh na gaavahee moorakh agiaanaa |
lines.english: Why do you not sing the Glorious Praises of the Lord, you ignorant fool?

lines.gurmukhi: ਝੂਠੈ ਲਾਲਚਿ ਲਾਗਿ ਕੈ ਨਹਿ ਮਰਨੁ ਪਛਾਨਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jhootthai laalach laag kai neh maran pachhaanaa |1|
lines.english: You are attached to false greed, and you do not even consider death. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਜਹੂ ਕਛੁ ਬਿਗਰਿਓ ਨਹੀ ਜੋ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਗੁਨ ਗਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: ajahoo kachh bigario nahee jo prabh gun gaavai |
lines.english: Even now, no harm has been done, if you will only sing God's Praises.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਹੁ ਨਾਨਕ ਤਿਹ ਭਜਨ ਤੇ ਨਿਰਭੈ ਪਦੁ ਪਾਵੈ ॥੨॥੧॥
lines.translit: kahu naanak tih bhajan te nirabhai pad paavai |2|1|
lines.english: Says Nanak, by meditating and vibrating upon Him, you shall obtain the state of fearlessness. ||2||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਲੰਗ ਮਹਲਾ ੯ ॥
lines.translit: tilang mahalaa 9 |
lines.english: Tilang, Ninth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਗ ਲੇਹੁ ਰੇ ਮਨਾ ਜਾਗ ਲੇਹੁ ਕਹਾ ਗਾਫਲ ਸੋਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: jaag lehu re manaa jaag lehu kahaa gaafal soeaa |
lines.english: Wake up, O mind! Wake up! Why are you sleeping unaware?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਤਨੁ ਉਪਜਿਆ ਸੰਗ ਹੀ ਸੋ ਭੀ ਸੰਗਿ ਨ ਹੋਇਆ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: jo tan upajiaa sang hee so bhee sang na hoeaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: That body, which you were born with, shall not go along with you in the end. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੀਉ ਛੂਟਿਓ ਜਬ ਦੇਹ ਤੇ ਡਾਰਿ ਅਗਨਿ ਮੈ ਦੀਨਾ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jeeo chhoottio jab deh te ddaar agan mai deenaa |1|
lines.english: will throw your body into the fire, when your soul departs from it. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਤ ਪਿਤਾ ਸੁਤ ਬੰਧ ਜਨ ਹਿਤੁ ਜਾ ਸਿਉ ਕੀਨਾ ॥
lines.translit: maat pitaa sut bandh jan hit jaa siau keenaa |
lines.english: Mother, father, children and relatives whom you love,
